topics.name: 100 Level: Foundation Computing and Natural Sciences
topics.description: COS 101, COS 102, General Studies, Mathematics, and Physics foundation courses.

subtopics.name: COS 101: Introduction to Computing Sciences
objectives: Trace history of computing, computer generations, and classification of computers
Identify computer hardware components, central processing unit CPU, and peripheral devices
Distinguish system software, operating systems, and application software

subtopics.name: COS 102: Problem Solving and Programming I
objectives: Formulate algorithms using flowcharts and pseudocode
Understand variables, data types, control structures (if-else, loops), and functions
Write basic programs in Python/C++ to solve computational problems

subtopics.name: MTH 101 & PHY 101: Mathematics and Physics for Computing
objectives: Apply algebra, trigonometry, coordinate geometry, and differential calculus
Understand mechanics, heat, electricity, magnetism, and wave motion principles

topics.name: 200 Level: Core Computer Science Fundamentals
topics.description: CSC 201 Programming II, Discrete Structures, Digital Logic, and Computer Architecture.

subtopics.name: CSC 201: Computer Programming II
objectives: Implement object-oriented programming concepts: classes, objects, encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism
Handle file I/O operations, exceptions, and recursion in C++/Java

subtopics.name: CSC 203: Discrete Structures and Logic
objectives: Apply set theory, mathematical logic, truth tables, and proof techniques
Analyze relations, functions, graph theory, trees, and combinatorics in computing

subtopics.name: CSC 204: Digital Logic Design and Assembly Language
objectives: Design logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR) and Boolean algebra simplification
Construct combinational circuits (adders, multiplexers) and sequential circuits (flip-flops, counters)
Understand computer organization, instruction set architecture (ISA), and assembly language programming

topics.name: 300 Level: Data Structures, Software Engineering, and SIWES
topics.description: CSC 301 Data Structures, Database Systems, Software Engineering, Operating Systems, and SIWES Training.

subtopics.name: CSC 301: Data Structures and Algorithms
objectives: Implement arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, binary search trees, and graphs
Analyze algorithm complexity using Big-O notation for searching (binary search) and sorting (quicksort, mergesort)

subtopics.name: CSC 302: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
objectives: Design relational databases using Entity-Relationship (ER) modeling and 1NF, 2NF, 3NF normalization
Write SQL queries for Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML)
Explain transaction processing, ACID properties, indexing, and database security

subtopics.name: CSC 304: Software Engineering & Operating Systems
objectives: Apply Agile and Waterfall software development life cycle (SDLC) methodologies
Understand process scheduling, memory management (virtual memory, paging), file systems, and concurrency/deadlocks

subtopics.name: CSC 399: SIWES (Students Industrial Work Experience Scheme)
objectives: Gain 6 months real-world software development and IT industry internship experience
Write technical industrial training report and defend work before departmental board

topics.name: 400 Level: Advanced Specializations and Final Year Project
topics.description: Artificial Intelligence, Computer Networks, Cybersecurity, Compiler Construction, and Research Project.

subtopics.name: CSC 401: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
objectives: Implement search algorithms (A* search, minimax) and knowledge representation
Understand supervised learning, unsupervised learning, neural networks, and deep learning basics

subtopics.name: CSC 402: Computer Networks and Cybersecurity
objectives: Explain OSI 7-layer model and TCP/IP protocol suite (IP routing, DNS, HTTP, TLS)
Understand network security threats, symmetric/asymmetrical cryptography (RSA, AES), and firewalls

subtopics.name: CSC 499: Final Year Research Project
objectives: Conduct independent original software development or theoretical computer science research
Write formal undergraduate thesis and present defense before external examiner
